item.title: I saved money by building a NAS-powered home surveillance system
item.itunes:title: I saved money by building a NAS-powered home surveillance system
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Opt for a middle-of-the-road home security setup with a Ring alarm, Synology NAS, and Reolink cameras for cost-effective surveillance.
  • The Synology DiskStation DS220+ offers great value with an Intel CPU, 2 drive bays, and up to 40TB storage. Perfect for DIY surveillance.
  • Easily set up Reolink cameras with Synology NAS for local storage. No need for monthly fees with a NAS enclosure and cameras with SD card slot.

Home security can range from a complete solution from an external company with a monthly subscription to a DIY job with hardware and a server. I've opted for somewhere in between with a Ring alarm system up and running with doors and hallways secured with sensors. I plan to complement this setup by adding some cameras around our home with units available from most tech stores and a NAS to record everything.

Picking a NAS and cameras

I decided to use a prebuilt NAS enclosure to store all recordings and manage IP cameras. I still have a few older models available for the job and the Synology DiskStation DS220+ was ideal with two drive bays, an Intel CPU, and a great OS with Synology's Surveillance Station ready to go. Synology provides two IP camera licenses with more available should you wish to hook up additional devices.

I already have an indoor camera as part of the Ring alarm system, so the plan was to add two external cameras. One is attached to the garage wall to monitor and record the driveway, and another is at the rear of the property. I had two old Reolink cameras that are no longer supported but should be easy enough to add to Synology's Surveillance Station with the latest firmware updates.

If you want to check out some of the brand's newer cameras, I would give the Reolink Argus Eco a consideration, especially with the internal battery pack and solar panel. It's essentially wire-free and painless to install anywhere on your property.

Synology DiskStation DS220+

Synology's DiskStation DS220+ is the company's most affordable two-bay with an Intel processor. You can upgrade the RAM, store up to 40TB of data, and enjoy the company's exceptionally good NAS OS.

$250 at Amazon $300 at Newegg
Reolink Argus Eco

Reolink Argus Eco is a budget-friendly outdoor security camera with a 2K recording resolution, motion sensor, and night vision, and can be connected to a solar panel to keep the internal battery topped up during the day.

$70 at Amazon

Setting up a surveillance station

Connecting the Reolink cameras to Synology's NAS was a breeze. First, I set up both cameras using the official Reolink mobile app, which then allowed me to configure the network settings. I logged into each camera's web GUI to update the firmware and make sure everything was running fine. Installing the latest versions of DSM and Surveillance Station on the DS220+ took around five minutes.

Being older cameras not officially supported by Reolink anymore, they still work remarkably well. The latest firmware was still available through official channels. Surveillance Station immediately picked them both up on the network and they were connected using credentials in seconds. The Reolink app is excellent and the company provides free cloud storage, but a NAS is better for local storage and keeping everything in-house.

Two 2TB IronWolf HDDs were installed inside the Synology DiskStation DS220+, which should provide ample space for storing hours of recorded footage. Both cameras are configured to record only when motion is detected to help limit how much data is saved on the enclosure.

Related
Best NAS devices in 2024
Expand your PC storage with one of these NAS enclosures

What do you need to build a NAS surveillance system?

You don't need a DVR or NAS to build a home surveillance system. Many brands such as Reolink make cameras and offer an app allowing you to manage and view footage. Many cameras will have SD card slots for storing footage that can be all you require when motion detection is enabled. Then there are paid subscriptions from these companies for cloud storage.

Where a NAS comes in handy is by taking your footage offline, keeping all your security recordings local, and saving you on monthly payments. If you do want to use a NAS, you'll need an enclosure, some NAS or surveillance-rated drives, and an IP camera or two. Synology makes it easy to get up and running with its free Surveillance Station app.

item.title: How to block spam callers on your phone
item.itunes:title: How to block spam callers on your phone
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Whether it's a telemarketer or someone impersonating the IRS, spam callers can take up quite a bit of your precious time, not to mention the cringe factor. Despite constant efforts from the FCC and carriers, spam calls remain a huge menace for most Americans.

While there's no foolproof solution to block all spam callers on your phone, you can take a few measures to ensure you don't receive as many spam calls as you do currently. I'll go over some settings you should immediately change on your Android phone or iPhone to block spam calls and highlight a few apps that will help you easily identify spam calls.

Related
How to change your sender or caller ID on iMessage and FaceTime
If you want to use a phone number or email address which is different than your actual information, Apple makes it easy to do this

How to block spam callers on Android

The default dialer app on most Android phones includes features that let you identify and filter spam calls or block specific phone numbers. You can enable these features to see a spam ID when you receive calls from a known spammer, filter suspected spam calls if you don't want to be disturbed, and completely block frequent spam callers.

Enable Spam ID and filtering using the Google Phone app

To see when you're getting a call from a known spammer or filter spam calls automatically, follow these steps:

  1. Open the Google Phone app.
  2. Select the three-dot menu button in the top right corner to open the context menu, and select the Settings option to access the app settings.
  3. On the Settings page, tap Caller ID & spam in the Assistive section to access the spam ID and filtering options.
  4. Enable the See caller and spam ID option if you want the Phone app to identify and flag calls from businesses and known spam callers.
  5. If you don't want your phone to ring when the Phone app identifies a spam caller, enable the Filter spam calls option as well.

The steps may vary slightly if you're not using the Google Phone app on your device, but you should find similar spam ID and filtering options in the default dialer app settings on most major Android phones. Install the Google Phone app if spam ID and filtering are not available in your device's default dialer.

The spam filtering option works well, but there's one downside. It may misidentify some calls as spam, and you might end up missing an important call. If you experience that after enabling the feature, you should turn it off and just rely on the spam ID feature to identify them so that you can choose to ignore spam callers.

Block specific spam callers using the Google Phone app

Although spammers usually call from random numbers, some may use the same few phone numbers to call you. If that's the case, you can follow these steps to block their phone number(s) specifically.

  1. Open the Google Phone app.
  2. Head to your call log by selecting the Recents option in the bottom bar.
  3. Long-press the number you wish to block and select the Block/report junk option from the drop-down menu.
  4. Check the Report call as spam option in the following pop-up and select Block.

You'll have to repeat these steps for all the phone numbers you wish to block. Once you're done, you won't receive any calls or texts from the blocked phone numbers. Suppose you unintentionally block a wrong phone number. In that case, you can unblock it by heading to the Google Phone app settings, selecting the Blocked numbers option in the General section, and tapping on the cross next to the phone number to unblock it.

Block calls from private or unidentified numbers using the Google Phone app

Some crafty spammers use private numbers to avoid detection and increase the chances of an unsuspecting user answering their call. If you're receiving such calls, you can enable the following setting to block all calls from private or unidentified numbers:

  1. Open the Google Phone app.
  2. Open the app settings by tapping the three-dot menu button in the top-right corner and selecting the Settings option from the context menu.
  3. Select the Blocked Numbers option in the General section.
  4. On the following page, enable the toggle next to the Unknown option.

While this option will block calls from completely private or unidentified phone numbers, it won't filter the calls coming in from phone numbers which simply aren't saved on your phone but are still visible on caller ID. Even though those numbers may be unknown to you, they are not considered to be completely unidentified callers which this option is designed to address.

How to block spam callers on iOS

Unlike Android, iOS doesn't have a built-in spam filtering feature specifically, but that doesn't mean you can't block spam callers on an iPhone. You can block calls from all unknown numbers on your iOS device to easily filter spam calls, but that might also block some legitimate callers and hide phone calls that you actually want to receive. If you don't want to risk missing an important call, you also have the option to block specific numbers individually.

Block all unknown callers on an iPhone

The most effective method to put an end to spam calls for good is to block calls from all unknown numbers, and although this puts you at risk of missing some welcome phone calls, iOS provides an easy way to do this.

  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Select Phone and tap the Silence Unknown Callers option on the following page.
  3. Tap the toggle next to the Silence Unknown Callers option to enable the feature.

Once enabled, you won't see incoming calls from unknown numbers, and the caller will be sent directly to voicemail. However, the silenced call will appear in your recent calls list. If the caller leaves a voicemail, it will also appear in your Visual Voicemail, like voicemails from known numbers.

The Silence Unknown Callers feature won't filter calls from numbers in your contacts. In addition, it won't filter numbers you've called recently and those you've interacted with in the Messages and Mail apps.

Block specific phone numbers on an iPhone

Like Android, iOS gives you the option to block calls and texts from specific phone numbers. This can be useful if you get repeated spam calls from the same few numbers. Follow these steps to block specific phone numbers on your iPhone:

  1. Open the Phone app.
  2. Select the Recents option from the bottom bar to open your call history.
  3. Scroll down to the number you want to block and tap the blue info button next to it to open the contact page.
  4. Tap Block this Caller on the contact page and select Block Contact on the following pop-up.

Blocking specific phone numbers in this manner will ensure you don't receive any calls, texts, or FaceTime calls from them. Calls from blocked numbers won't appear in the recent calls list, and all voicemails will be saved in the Blocked Messages folder at the bottom of the Visual Voicemail list.

Use third-party apps to block spam callers on Android and iOS

If the built-in spam filtering features on Android and iOS don't help, you can try third-party apps to block spam callers. All major carriers in the U.S. offer handy spam-blocking apps. While these apps are free, AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon have paid premium plans that give you access to extras like reverse number lookup, proxy phone numbers, and enhanced caller ID. However, you should be able to steer clear of the paid versions unless you really need those features.

Download the spam-blocking app from your carrier by following the links below and follow the on-screen instructions to block spam callers.

1. AT&T ActiveArmor

  • Android
  • iOS

2. T-Mobile Scam Shield

  • Android
  • iOS

3. Verizon Call Filter

  • Android
  • iOS

Google Fi also has a spam filtering feature, but you don't need to install a separate app to use it. Subscribers can block known spam callers by navigating to the Privacy & security section in the Account tab of the Google Fi Wireless app, selecting the Manage contact settings option under the Spam and Contact Settings section, and enabling the Block Known Spam option.

The Google Play Store and Apple App Store also have several other spam call filtering apps, but proceed with caution. Apps like Truecaller, Call Blocker, and Should I Answer? are great alternatives to dedicated apps from carriers. However, apps like these require access to your contacts, call history, and other sensitive data to work as intended and have been caught in the past sharing this data with third parties and putting users at risk due to a data breach. Therefore, we wouldn't recommend using them unless all the aforementioned options don't work.

Decrease the number of spam calls you receive daily

Enabling the settings mentioned above or using the recommended spam-blocking apps won't completely block spam callers on your phone, but it should ensure you don't receive as many spam calls as you do right now. It's definitely worth the effort, and I highly recommend you give it a shot if you're tired of the growing spam call menace.

While you're at it, you can also register for the FCC's National Do Not Call Registry to get fewer telemarketing calls on your phone. Also, check out our guide on how to block spam messages on Android and iOS to prevent spammers from reaching you over text.

  3. Scroll down to the number you want to block and tap the blue info button next to it to open the contact page.
  4. Tap Block this Caller on the contact page and select Block Contact on the following pop-up.

Blocking specific phone numbers in this manner will ensure you don't receive any calls, texts, or FaceTime calls from them. Calls from blocked numbers won't appear in the recent calls list, and all voicemails will be saved in the Blocked Messages folder at the bottom of the Visual Voicemail list.

Use third-party apps to block spam callers on Android and iOS

If the built-in spam filtering features on Android and iOS don't help, you can try third-party apps to block spam callers. All major carriers in the U.S. offer handy spam-blocking apps. While these apps are free, AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon have paid premium plans that give you access to extras like reverse number lookup, proxy phone numbers, and enhanced caller ID. However, you should be able to steer clear of the paid versions unless you really need those features.

Download the spam-blocking app from your carrier by following the links below and follow the on-screen instructions to block spam callers.

1. AT&T ActiveArmor

  • Android
  • iOS

2. T-Mobile Scam Shield

  • Android
  • iOS

3. Verizon Call Filter

  • Android
  • iOS

Google Fi also has a spam filtering feature, but you don't need to install a separate app to use it. Subscribers can block known spam callers by navigating to the Privacy & security section in the Account tab of the Google Fi Wireless app, selecting the Manage contact settings option under the Spam and Contact Settings section, and enabling the Block Known Spam option.

The Google Play Store and Apple App Store also have several other spam call filtering apps, but proceed with caution. Apps like Truecaller, Call Blocker, and Should I Answer? are great alternatives to dedicated apps from carriers. However, apps like these require access to your contacts, call history, and other sensitive data to work as intended and have been caught in the past sharing this data with third parties and putting users at risk due to a data breach. Therefore, we wouldn't recommend using them unless all the aforementioned options don't work.

Decrease the number of spam calls you receive daily

Enabling the settings mentioned above or using the recommended spam-blocking apps won't completely block spam callers on your phone, but it should ensure you don't receive as many spam calls as you do right now. It's definitely worth the effort, and I highly recommend you give it a shot if you're tired of the growing spam call menace.

While you're at it, you can also register for the FCC's National Do Not Call Registry to get fewer telemarketing calls on your phone. Also, check out our guide on how to block spam messages on Android and iOS to prevent spammers from reaching you over text.
item.content:encoded:
Whether it's a telemarketer or someone impersonating the IRS, spam callers can take up quite a bit of your precious time, not to mention the cringe factor. Despite constant efforts from the FCC and carriers, spam calls remain a huge menace for most Americans.

While there's no foolproof solution to block all spam callers on your phone, you can take a few measures to ensure you don't receive as many spam calls as you do currently. I'll go over some settings you should immediately change on your Android phone or iPhone to block spam calls and highlight a few apps that will help you easily identify spam calls.

Related
How to change your sender or caller ID on iMessage and FaceTime
If you want to use a phone number or email address which is different than your actual information, Apple makes it easy to do this

How to block spam callers on Android

The default dialer app on most Android phones includes features that let you identify and filter spam calls or block specific phone numbers. You can enable these features to see a spam ID when you receive calls from a known spammer, filter suspected spam calls if you don't want to be disturbed, and completely block frequent spam callers.

Enable Spam ID and filtering using the Google Phone app

To see when you're getting a call from a known spammer or filter spam calls automatically, follow these steps:

  1. Open the Google Phone app.
  2. Select the three-dot menu button in the top right corner to open the context menu, and select the Settings option to access the app settings.
  3. On the Settings page, tap Caller ID & spam in the Assistive section to access the spam ID and filtering options.
  4. Enable the See caller and spam ID option if you want the Phone app to identify and flag calls from businesses and known spam callers.
  5. If you don't want your phone to ring when the Phone app identifies a spam caller, enable the Filter spam calls option as well.

The steps may vary slightly if you're not using the Google Phone app on your device, but you should find similar spam ID and filtering options in the default dialer app settings on most major Android phones. Install the Google Phone app if spam ID and filtering are not available in your device's default dialer.

The spam filtering option works well, but there's one downside. It may misidentify some calls as spam, and you might end up missing an important call. If you experience that after enabling the feature, you should turn it off and just rely on the spam ID feature to identify them so that you can choose to ignore spam callers.

Block specific spam callers using the Google Phone app

Although spammers usually call from random numbers, some may use the same few phone numbers to call you. If that's the case, you can follow these steps to block their phone number(s) specifically.

  1. Open the Google Phone app.
  2. Head to your call log by selecting the Recents option in the bottom bar.
  3. Long-press the number you wish to block and select the Block/report junk option from the drop-down menu.
  4. Check the Report call as spam option in the following pop-up and select Block.

You'll have to repeat these steps for all the phone numbers you wish to block. Once you're done, you won't receive any calls or texts from the blocked phone numbers. Suppose you unintentionally block a wrong phone number. In that case, you can unblock it by heading to the Google Phone app settings, selecting the Blocked numbers option in the General section, and tapping on the cross next to the phone number to unblock it.

Block calls from private or unidentified numbers using the Google Phone app

Some crafty spammers use private numbers to avoid detection and increase the chances of an unsuspecting user answering their call. If you're receiving such calls, you can enable the following setting to block all calls from private or unidentified numbers:

  1. Open the Google Phone app.
  2. Open the app settings by tapping the three-dot menu button in the top-right corner and selecting the Settings option from the context menu.
  3. Select the Blocked Numbers option in the General section.
  4. On the following page, enable the toggle next to the Unknown option.

While this option will block calls from completely private or unidentified phone numbers, it won't filter the calls coming in from phone numbers which simply aren't saved on your phone but are still visible on caller ID. Even though those numbers may be unknown to you, they are not considered to be completely unidentified callers which this option is designed to address.

How to block spam callers on iOS

Unlike Android, iOS doesn't have a built-in spam filtering feature specifically, but that doesn't mean you can't block spam callers on an iPhone. You can block calls from all unknown numbers on your iOS device to easily filter spam calls, but that might also block some legitimate callers and hide phone calls that you actually want to receive. If you don't want to risk missing an important call, you also have the option to block specific numbers individually.

Block all unknown callers on an iPhone

The most effective method to put an end to spam calls for good is to block calls from all unknown numbers, and although this puts you at risk of missing some welcome phone calls, iOS provides an easy way to do this.

  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Select Phone and tap the Silence Unknown Callers option on the following page.
  3. Tap the toggle next to the Silence Unknown Callers option to enable the feature.

Once enabled, you won't see incoming calls from unknown numbers, and the caller will be sent directly to voicemail. However, the silenced call will appear in your recent calls list. If the caller leaves a voicemail, it will also appear in your Visual Voicemail, like voicemails from known numbers.

The Silence Unknown Callers feature won't filter calls from numbers in your contacts. In addition, it won't filter numbers you've called recently and those you've interacted with in the Messages and Mail apps.

Block specific phone numbers on an iPhone

Like Android, iOS gives you the option to block calls and texts from specific phone numbers. This can be useful if you get repeated spam calls from the same few numbers. Follow these steps to block specific phone numbers on your iPhone:

  1. Open the Phone app.
  2. Select the Recents option from the bottom bar to open your call history.
  3. Scroll down to the number you want to block and tap the blue info button next to it to open the contact page.
  4. Tap Block this Caller on the contact page and select Block Contact on the following pop-up.

Blocking specific phone numbers in this manner will ensure you don't receive any calls, texts, or FaceTime calls from them. Calls from blocked numbers won't appear in the recent calls list, and all voicemails will be saved in the Blocked Messages folder at the bottom of the Visual Voicemail list.

Use third-party apps to block spam callers on Android and iOS

If the built-in spam filtering features on Android and iOS don't help, you can try third-party apps to block spam callers. All major carriers in the U.S. offer handy spam-blocking apps. While these apps are free, AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon have paid premium plans that give you access to extras like reverse number lookup, proxy phone numbers, and enhanced caller ID. However, you should be able to steer clear of the paid versions unless you really need those features.

Download the spam-blocking app from your carrier by following the links below and follow the on-screen instructions to block spam callers.

1. AT&T ActiveArmor

  • Android
  • iOS

2. T-Mobile Scam Shield

  • Android
  • iOS

3. Verizon Call Filter

  • Android
  • iOS

Google Fi also has a spam filtering feature, but you don't need to install a separate app to use it. Subscribers can block known spam callers by navigating to the Privacy & security section in the Account tab of the Google Fi Wireless app, selecting the Manage contact settings option under the Spam and Contact Settings section, and enabling the Block Known Spam option.

The Google Play Store and Apple App Store also have several other spam call filtering apps, but proceed with caution. Apps like Truecaller, Call Blocker, and Should I Answer? are great alternatives to dedicated apps from carriers. However, apps like these require access to your contacts, call history, and other sensitive data to work as intended and have been caught in the past sharing this data with third parties and putting users at risk due to a data breach. Therefore, we wouldn't recommend using them unless all the aforementioned options don't work.

Decrease the number of spam calls you receive daily

Enabling the settings mentioned above or using the recommended spam-blocking apps won't completely block spam callers on your phone, but it should ensure you don't receive as many spam calls as you do right now. It's definitely worth the effort, and I highly recommend you give it a shot if you're tired of the growing spam call menace.

While you're at it, you can also register for the FCC's National Do Not Call Registry to get fewer telemarketing calls on your phone. Also, check out our guide on how to block spam messages on Android and iOS to prevent spammers from reaching you over text.

item.title: Never skimp on these peripherals for your PC
item.itunes:title: Never skimp on these peripherals for your PC
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
You can spend all the time and money you want putting together your dream PC, but you're not going to get the best experience and will be left wanting for more without the right peripherals. Think of your PC like a high-performance sports car, which is armed to the teeth to handle the tracks. But you're not going to have the best experience, or even the confidence to drive and push it to its limits without the right tires and things like a comfortable seat.

Similarly, you're not going to tap into your PC's full potential until you get your hands on the right peripherals. The "deals" and affordable pricing on low-quality products from lesser known brands may seem lucrative given all the money you may have spent putting together the PC, but be warned that skimping on peripherals for your high-end PC is just false economy. That being said, here are five PC peripherals that you should never skimp on to ensure you're having a good computing experience, be it for work or gaming.

5 Keyboard

That one thing you use more than anything

We arguably use our keyboard more than anything while sitting in front of a computer, regardless of whether it is for work or for playing games. Yet, I've seen so many settling for cheap and mushy keyboards, compromising on both comfort and efficiency. Buying a low-quality keyboard is like picking up the recipe to brew frustration, in my opinion. No, seriously, take it from someone who's coming from using low-quality, affordable keyboards to typing on a custom mechanical keyboard now. They're as different as night and day, and typing on a keyboard with crisp and responsive keys that register every press effortlessly is something that'll be hard to go back from.

I know building a custom mechanical keyboard from scratch is not everyone's cup of tea, but the least you can do is put the same amount of time and effort in understanding all the options out there to figure out what's best for you instead of simply picking up a prebuilt keyboard. It's also about ergonomics as much as it is about the typing feel because a keyboard with good ergonomics can prevent wrist strain and keep you comfortable during those long work sessions or gaming marathons. So the next time you're tempted to pick up a keyboard from the bargain-basement, just remember that your fingers deserve better.

Related
Best keyboards in 2024
There are countless keyboards on the market, but you only want one. We can help.

4 Mousepad

Because simply buying the best mouse isn't enough

You may have heard how important it is to buy the right mouse for your setup, as it is an extension of your hand. But what most people don't tell you is how even the best mouse is often pulled back by the subpar surface it is on. Buying one of the cheap mousepads may seem like a harmless cost-cutting measure, but I'm here to tell you that it's the worst thing you can do to impact your performance and comfort. A good-quality mousepad gives you a smooth and consistent surface to effortlessly glide your pointer.

It's crucial to have a good mousepad because using your mouse on a bumpy or an uneven surface will cause mouse jitters, forcing you to battle lag and inconsistency. Using a good mousepad, on the other hand, translates to improved tracking accuracy, faster response times, and a more enjoyable overall experience. It's also crucial to pick large mousepads as they provide ample space for sweeping motions, reducing wrist strain and fatigue during long gaming sessions or workdays. Notably, you must also pay attention to the materials and the overall quality of the mousepad to ensure they offer good grip, preventing the mousepad from slipping around on your desk.

Related
Best mousepads in 2024
These are the best mousepads for maximizing the precision and control of your mouse movements.

3 Headphones

Imagine pouring your heart and soul into building a killer PC, with top-of-the-line internals and a stunning monitor for all your media; but then you fire your favorite game or play your favorite soundtrack, and the sound is… underwhelming. Explosions lack punch, dialogue sounds muffled, and the immersive soundtrack is lost in a sea of mediocrity. A good PC setup is just not complete without a good pair of headphones. Sound is extremely crucial while consuming digital content, and it's literally the difference between watching a slideshow and being transported to a new world.

It's just as crucial for productivity as it is for media consumption and gaming, as a lot of modern headphones have active noise cancelation or good sound isolation to help you get through open-office environments or noisy roommates. A high-quality pair of headphones with active noise-canceling is one of the best additions I've made to my PC setup, as it allows me to block out all the distractions and maximize productivity. You don't necessarily have to buy an expensive pair of cans with all the bells and whistles, though. Just look for something that helps you experience rich audio and be more focused while working to form an enjoyable connection with your PC.

Related
Best noise-canceling headphones in 2024
Silence your surroundings, so you can focus on your favorite music, movies, and podcasts.

2 Chair

Your PC's unsung hero

The chair of your PC setup, which is usually an afterthought for most people, is arguably the most crucial addition you can make to your setup. This is particularly true for those who spend hours sitting in front of a computer, be it for long gaming sessions or work. A cheap and poorly designed chair with inadequate back support becomes a breeding ground for aches and pains. It's time you looked at the chair as an investment in your health, rather than just a surface to sit on. I say that because poor posture promoted by a bad quality chair can turn computing into a source of frustration for you, causing neck strain, back problems, and even headaches.

It's important to ensure your body remains aligned during the long hours you spend glued to the screen. I suggest trying a couple of options and picking the one that suits you best. You can also look for chairs that offer things like adjustable armrests and lumbar support for maximum comfort. You may have to spend a good amount of money to buy a good quality chair for your setup, but I'd say this one — in particular — is worth it because, well, it may quite literally save your back.

Related
FlexiSpot C7 Ergonomic Office Chair review: For those who want lumbar support, and a lot of it
The FlexiSpot C7 Ergonomic Chair is designed to reduce back pain and facilitate good posture, but its aggressive lumbar support won't be for everyone.

1 Monitor

One that significantly impacts your experience

Buying a low-quality monitor is one of the worst things you can do for your setup. A monitor is essentially your window to the digital world, and staring at a sub-par monitor is like peering through a dusty window. As someone who recently switched to a relatively high-end gaming monitor from a basic 1080p monitor, I can't recommend this particular upgrade enough. Not only is it crucial for gamers and creative professionals to have the best monitors with top-of-the-line specifications, but it's also equally important for those who tackle everyday office and school work.

A good monitor goes beyond basic functionality, as high-resolution displays with vibrant colors and deep blacks create an immersive experience, drawing you deeper into the action. It's also crucial to pay attention to features like wide viewing angles, as they all play a key role in improving your overall experience, during extended work and gaming sessions. Investing in a quality monitor ensures you see the digital world in all its glory, with crisp details, smooth visuals, and vibrant colors. So, don't let a subpar monitor hold you back, and unlock the full potential of your PC.

Related
Best monitors in 2024
Need a new monitor for your desktop setup? These are some of the best monitors you can buy right now for productivity and entertainment.

Unlock your PC's full potential

So, there you have it! Those are some peripherals that I highly recommend splurging on. The initial cost of quality peripherals might seem daunting, but remember that they're an investment in your long-term PC experience. Pick the right peripherals and say goodbye to frustration from unresponsive keyboards, sluggish mice, or backaches from flimsy chairs.

Buying a low-quality monitor is one of the worst things you can do for your setup. A monitor is essentially your window to the digital world, and staring at a sub-par monitor is like peering through a dusty window. As someone who recently switched to a relatively high-end gaming monitor from a basic 1080p monitor, I can't recommend this particular upgrade enough. Not only is it crucial for gamers and creative professionals to have the best monitors with top-of-the-line specifications, but it's also equally important for those who tackle everyday office and school work.

A good monitor goes beyond basic functionality, as high-resolution displays with vibrant colors and deep blacks create an immersive experience, drawing you deeper into the action. It's also crucial to pay attention to features like wide viewing angles, as they all play a key role in improving your overall experience, during extended work and gaming sessions. Investing in a quality monitor ensures you see the digital world in all its glory, with crisp details, smooth visuals, and vibrant colors. So, don't let a subpar monitor hold you back, and unlock the full potential of your PC.

Related
Best monitors in 2024
Need a new monitor for your desktop setup? These are some of the best monitors you can buy right now for productivity and entertainment.

Unlock your PC's full potential

So, there you have it! Those are some peripherals that I highly recommend splurging on. The initial cost of quality peripherals might seem daunting, but remember that they're an investment in your long-term PC experience. Pick the right peripherals and say goodbye to frustration from unresponsive keyboards, sluggish mice, or backaches from flimsy chairs.

item.title: How to set up or transfer an eSIM on iPhone
item.itunes:title: How to set up or transfer an eSIM on iPhone
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Setting up a new iPhone or transferring your data from an old model to a new one is typically a simple process. But one part can often be frustrating: swapping SIM cards. Enter the eSIM, a digital SIM card that removes the need for a minuscule card that has to be installed properly. An eSIM works with any iPhone XS, XS Max, iPhone XR, or newer, as long as the phone is being used with a wireless carrier or provider that supports it. Some phones are moving away from physical SIM cards altogether. You should be able to install eight or more eSIMs to a single phone if desired, even using two different phone numbers at the same time.

18:04
Related
iPhone 15 Pro Max review: Apple is slowly winning over this Android diehard
The iPhone 15 Pro Max may not look much different from the iPhone 14 Pro Max, but it brings much improved cameras and ergonomics

What is an eSIM?

An eSIM is, as the name implies, an electronic version of the old physical SIM cards of yesteryear (although those are still very much around). Instead of being a tiny little card you insert into a slot on the phone, an eSIM is embedded in the phone itself, making it easy to swap its information from one model to another. Apple has supported eSIMs since back with the iPhone XR, but starting with the iPhone 14 (and continuing with the iPhone 15 series), the company removed the SIM card slot altogether for versions sold in the U.S. (versions in other countries still support both formats).

If you travel, you'll be pleased to know that newer iPhones support up to eight eSIMs, so you can use a plan from another country when you are traveling without having to switch devices or swap out physical cards. Just make sure not to activate that eSIM until you are physically in that country.

How to set up an eSIM on iPhone

If you’re setting up an eSIM on one of the best iPhones, you'll have the option to activate an eSIM during setup as long as the iPhone is connected to a Wi-Fi network and the carrier has assigned an eSIM to the iPhone at the time of purchase.

When the carrier has activated the plan, you will see a notice that says Carrier Cellular Plan Ready to Be Installed. Select it. Under Settings, select Carrier Cellular Plan Ready to Be Installed and tap Continue at the bottom of the screen. Simply follow the instructions from there.

There’s also an eSIM Quick Transfer option for transferring a SIM from a previous iPhone to a new one without involving your carrier. This method is ideal if you bought a used phone or one outside of your current carrier. If you're switching from Android to iPhone, you will need your carrier to transfer the phone number using eSIM Carrier Activation.

If you do not see the option to set up an eSIM while setting up your new phone, contact your carrier for activation or use the QR code provided in the carrier’s iPhone app.

To test the transfer, make a phone call. If it isn’t working, contact your carrier for further support. Once you have verified that everything works, you can remove the old physical SIM card from the phone (if it has one) and restart it.

How to transfer an eSIM on an iPhone

If you are moving from one iPhone to another, you might be able to handle the transfer process without the assistance of your carrier.

  1. Turn on both iPhones and make sure they are both connected to the same Wi-Fi network.
  2. On the new iPhone, go to Settings > Cellular.
  3. Select Add eSIM.
  4. From there, you’ll see Set Up Cellular, where you can transfer a phone number from a nearby iPhone or use a QR code provided by your wireless carrier.
  5. Choose the method you want and follow the instructions that appear on your old iPhone. You may be asked to input a verification code which will show up on the new iPhone. You may also be redirected to the wireless carrier’s page to transfer the eSIM there. If so, and you require further assistance, contact your carrier.

Once you have set up an eSIM on a new iPhone, wait for the cellular plan to activate. Once it is active, the old SIM card will automatically be deactivated, and you can enter the new world of virtual SIM cards.

Some carriers offer an option to convert a physical SIM card to an eSIM on the same iPhone. If you see the Convert to eSIM option under Cellular, this means the carrier supports this option, and you can proceed with the steps above.

eSIMs are the future

eSIMs are the future of SIM cards, and they make sense. If you have ever had to rummage in the drawer for the little tool that comes with the iPhone to help you remove a physical SIM card (or perhaps you've fashioned a paper clip into one), you know the frustration. Never mind having to figure out the right way to insert it! eSIMs make everything electronic and virtual so that there's no physical product involved in the transfer. It also makes it easier to switch and to load multiple numbers to a single device, ideal for business owners and travelers. Whatever the reason, once you're ready to set up an eSIM or transfer it to a fancy new phone like the iPhone 15 Pro Max, you might be able to do so without any assistance from your carrier, as long as everything is set up correctly in advance.

item.title: 4 ways to fix "Nvidia installer cannot continue" error on Windows
item.itunes:title: 4 ways to fix "Nvidia installer cannot continue" error on Windows
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Nvidia's GeForce Experience is one of the most useful utilities you can install on your Nvidia-powered gaming PC. It offers a lot of useful features to improve your overall gaming experience, and it also makes it easy to install driver updates. The GeForce Experience software is not without its flaws, though, and you may occasionally hit a wall trying to use it on your PC. It's particularly known to throw some errors while installing a driver update, like the one we're trying to solve here. If you're seeing the "Nvidia installer cannot continue" on your PC and are wondering what to do, then read on to find some possible ways to fix it.

Related
How to update the graphics driver on your PC
Keeping your GPU drivers up to date is essential to get the best performance in the latest games. Here's how to do it.

Disable and re-enable automatic downloads

Forcing the client for a fresh download

If you see the "Nvidia installation cannot continue" error, then there's a good chance that the graphics driver that you're trying to install wasn't downloaded properly. You can try to fix this by forcing GeForce Experience to download the driver again by disabling and re-enabling automatic downloads. To do this:

  1. Open the GeForce Experience client on your PC, and click the settings cog icon to enter the settings page.
  2. Ensure you are on the General settings page by clicking the General tab on the left sidebar.
  3. Scroll down till you find the Automatically download driver updates and let me choose when to install option under the Downloads tab, and uncheck the box to disable it.
  4. Close the client, and follow steps 1 to 3, except you'll check the box this time to enable it again.

Run the downloaded file manually from File Explorer

Take matters in your own hands

If the automatically downloaded driver fails to install on your PC and keeps throwing the "Nvidia installer cannot continue" error, then you can try executing the downloaded update file manually using the Windows File Explorer. To do this:

  1. Ensure GeForce Experience has downloaded the driver update file. You can confirm this by opening the GeForce Experience client and checking whether there's a driver that's available to install under the Drivers tab.
  2. Open File Explorer on your Windows PC, and head to Nvidia's installation directory to find the downloaded file.
  3. By default, you'll find it in C:\ProgramData\NVIDIA Corporation\Downloader.
  4. Look for the folders with unusual names, and open them to locate the downloaded driver files.
  5. Ensure you've located the latest driver by checking the first 6 letters and matching it with pop-up on the GeForce Experience client.
  6. Double-click the file to run it manually, and let it install.

Reinstall GeForce Experience

Simply reinstall to fix the underlying problem

If disabling and re-enabling automatic driver updates in GeForce Experience didn't work, then you might want to try reinstalling the GeForce Experience client itself. Removing the client entirely isn't an ideal solution, but simply reinstalling a program is known to fix a lot of its underlying problems, so consider doing this if you've run out of options, and don't mind logging in and setting up GeForce Experience again. Follow the steps below to remove and reinstall GeForce Experience on your Windows PC.

Removing GeForce Experience may reset your settings along with the shortcuts you may have assigned in the client.

  1. Press Win + I keys on your keyboard to enter Windows Settings, and click the Apps option from the left sidebar.
  2. Click the Installed Apps option.
  3. Use the search bar on the top to locate the GeForce Experience client.
  4. Click the three-dot icon next to the GeForce Experience listing, and select the Uninstall option.
  5. Select Uninstall again to confirm your selection to remove the current version of GeForce Experience from your PC.
  6. Head to Nvidia's download page to fetch the latest version of the GeForce Experience client.
  7. Run the downloaded setup file to install the client, and then login when prompted to complete the setup.
  8. Follow the steps highlighted in one of the sections above to enable automatic driver downloads to ensure your PC downloads the drivers automatically.

Update your graphics driver manually

A tried-and-tested fix

If you don't want to reinstall GeForce Experience, then you can also update the Nvidia graphics driver manually to see if it fixes it. This particular method involves a lot of steps, but it's known to fix this particular issue that's likely caused due to a GeForce Experience error. To manually install your Nvidia graphics card drivers:

  1. Go to Nvidia's driver download center on its official website.
  2. Select the required fields for GPU, your OS, and the correct type of drivers — Game Ready or Studio Driver.
  3. Click Search to find the latest driver available for your PC.
  4. Click Download to begin downloading the driver.
  5. Run the installer on your PC and follow the on-screen instructions.
  6. Choose whether to install the Graphics Driver and GeForce Experience or just the driver itself. Click Agree and continue.
  7. Choose the Express install to go with default settings or the Custom install if you want to run the installation with some custom settings.
  8. Follow the on-screen instructions, wait for the installation to finish, and click Close.

Get your Nvidia drivers installed smoothly

That brings us to the end of this guide in which we've highlighted some tried-and-tested troubleshooting methods to fix the "Nvidia installer cannot continue" error on Windows. This particular error typically shows up when the driver fails to download properly. There are plenty of different ways to fix it, and we hope at least one of the aforementioned troubleshooting methods worked for you. With that out of the way, please make sure to keep your graphics driver updated all the time for smooth functioning of your PC and the graphics card inside it.

That brings us to the end of this guide in which we've highlighted some tried-and-tested troubleshooting methods to fix the "Nvidia installer cannot continue" error on Windows. This particular error typically shows up when the driver fails to download properly. There are plenty of different ways to fix it, and we hope at least one of the aforementioned troubleshooting methods worked for you. With that out of the way, please make sure to keep your graphics driver updated all the time for smooth functioning of your PC and the graphics card inside it.

item.title: How to get an iPhone out of SOS only mode
item.itunes:title: How to get an iPhone out of SOS only mode
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
If you have an iPhone stuck in SOS Only mode, then your phone can't find a reliable network. Even the best iPhones can encounter this problem. Thankfully, you typically can resolve the issue with just a few simple steps.

What to know about SOS or SOS only mode

When a phone is in SOS or SOS Only mode, it isn’t connected to a cellular network and is actively searching for one. You can still make emergency calls within the U.S., Canada, and Australia, but otherwise your phone functionality is severely limited. While in SOS or SOS Only mode, your iPhone can temporarily connect to nearby Wi-Fi networks when available, which will let you access instant messaging and video chatting apps, surf the web, and stream music and video. However, until your phone exits these modes, you won’t be able to make or receive phone calls over a cellular network, nor can you receive SMS text messages.

SOS or SOS Only mode can crop up in a wide variety of iPhones. Newer phones, such as the iPhone 15 Pro Max and models that run on iOS 17, are just as vulnerable as the iPhone 5S or 5c, although usually for different reasons.

Restart your iPhone

The simplest solution is often the best, and you can't get any simpler than a quick restart. This process refreshes and frees up the device's memory, which lets programs start anew. However, there are different methods to restart various iPhone models.

Source: Pexels

Follow these steps if you have an iPhone X, 11, 12, 13, or later:

1. Press and hold the side button and either the volume up or volume down button until the Power Off slider appears.

2. Drag the slider to the other side of the screen.

3. Wait for your iPhone to turn off.

4. Once your phone is off, press and hold the right-side button to turn it back on.

If you own an iPhone 6, 7, 8, or SE 2 or 3, use these instructions:

1. Press and hold the right-side button until the Power Off slider appears.

2. Drag the slider to the other side of the screen.

3. Wait until your iPhone turns off.

4. Press and hold the right-side button to turn your phone on again.

Use the following instructions if you use a first-gen iPhone SE, iPhone 5 or earlier:

1. Press and hold the top button to get the Power Off slider.

2. Drag the slider to the other side of the screen.

3. Wait for your phone to turn off.

4. After your phone powers down, press and hold the top button to turn it on.

Nine times out of ten, restarting your iPhone will actually clear up the issue. However, if the oft-reliable method of "have you tried turning it off and on again" doesn't work, you will need to try something else.

Troubleshoot your iPhone in SOS only mode

If restarting your phone doesn't work, the problem might lie with your cellular network or your iPhone's SIM card or eSim setup; or to be more specific, the lack thereof. First, make sure you are within an active cellular network and that your phone's SIM or eSIM was activated by a carrier with a plan. You can usually do this by contacting your carrier. Check to ensure your personal account is still active, the network is up and running, and your phone hasn't been blocked.

Source: Unsplash

If you just bought a new phone, check out our guide on how to set up iPhone 15, which includes all the necessary steps for setting up any iPhone. If these parameters are met and the phone is still in SOS mode, you can try a few more solutions.

Turn off your 3G

One cause of SOS and SOS Only mode is the outdated 3G network. As more carriers phase out 3G, the phones which can still use it are encountering more connectivity issues, resulting in SOS and SOS Only mode. While most modern iPhones don't have to worry about this, models such as the iPhone 6 can encounter this problem. In order to fix this issue, you have to get your iPhone off 3G, which may not always be possible, but see if you can do the following:

1. Update your phone if needed.

2. Tap Cellular Data Options.

3. Turn on Enable LTE.

When told to ignore 3G networks, iPhones have significantly fewer connectivity issues, thereby reducing the odds of entering SOS or SOS Only mode. However, if the problem persists, contact your cellular provider.

Make sure your iPhone is not in Airplane Mode

If your iPhone is in Airplane Mode, all connectivity is temporarily disabled. Many people use this mode while traveling by air, but it can also conserve battery life in a pinch. You might have even enabled Airplane Mode by accident, but you can easily check and turn it off:

1. Pull down from the Home Screen to open Control Center.

2. Check the Airplane Mode icon. If it is on, tap the symbol to turn Airplane Mode off (it should be gray).

Alternatively, you can use your iPhone's Airplane Mode to reset its cellular network connection without restarting the phone:

1. Open the phone's Control Center.

2. Tap the Airplane Mode icon.

3. Leave the iPhone in Airplane Mode for at least 15 seconds.

4. Tap the Airplane Mode icon again to turn it off.

If your iPhone was stuck in Airplane Mode or needed a cellular connection reset, the SOS or SOS Only mode should disappear when it resumes normal functions.

Also note, you can alternatively access Airplane Mode through your iPhone Settings, where you can toggle it off or on there as needed for the steps above.

Turn on your iPhone's cellular data

Even if your iPhone isn't in Airplane Mode, it might be stuck in SOS or SOS Only mode because its cellular data has been deactivated. Follow these steps to make sure that isn't the case:

1. Go to Settings.

2. Select Cellular.

3. Make sure Cellular Data is on. If not, tap the slider to turn it on.

4. If Cellular Data is already on, tap the slider anyway to turn it off, pause, then tap it again to turn Cellular Data on again.

Once you are done, the Cellular Data slider should turn green. Whether you have turned it on or reset the connection, SOS or SOS Only mode should go away.

Turn on your iPhone's data roaming

If you're traveling internationally and don't have data roaming set up, your phone might enter SOS or SOS Only mode. If this happens, activating data roaming should clear up the problem.

However, the steps you should take below will depend on how many SIM cards you use.

If your iPhone has only one SIM card or eSIM then follow these instructions:

1. Go to Settings.

2. Select Cellular or Cellular Data.

3. Make sure Cellular Data is turned on.

4. Tap Cellular Data Options.

5. Set Data Roaming to on.

Or if your phone uses dual SIM and/or eSIM you won't see the same options as listed above, so follow these instructions instead:

1. Go to Settings.

2. Tap on Cellular or Cellular Data.

3. Make sure that Cellular Data is turned on.

4. Find the SIMs section, then tap on the number you want to use Data Roaming with.

5. Tap the slider to activate Data Roaming on that line.

While data roaming should fix SOS and SOS Only issues while abroad, please note that roaming charges will apply. Check plan options with your carrier ahead of time or call them immediately to confirm.

Update your iPhone's carrier settings

Sometimes, an iPhone's carrier settings can cause issues with your connection, resulting in your phone entering SOS or SOS Only mode. If this is the case, an update can usually fix the problem:

1. Connect your phone to a Wi-Fi network.

2. Select Settings.

3. Select General.

4. Select About. If a carrier update is available, an option will appear in this menu.

5. Download the new carrier settings patch.

If the SOS or SOS Only issue stemmed from carrier settings, your phone should connect back to networks after the update is installed. Please note that if you insert a new SIM card or eSIM into your iPhone, you will need to reinstall the latest carrier settings update.

Verify your iPhone software is up-to-date

If your iPhone's carrier settings aren't to blame, then something in the phone's software could be preventing a proper network connection, thus causing the SOS or SOS Only mode. If that happens, a software update can clear things up:

1. Connect your iPhone to a Wi-Fi network.

2. Go to your phone's Settings.

3. Select General.

4. Select Software Update.

5. If your phone is running the latest software, you don't need to do anything else. If an update is available, select Update Now and download the patch.

Once your iPhone has finished installing the update and restarted, it should be able to reconnect to networks as normal, thus solving the issue.

Disable or enable automatic network selection

Many phones, iPhone included, can pick the proper network for users. This feature, known as automatic network selection, can occasionally backfire if it doesn't find the best network immediately, thus causing iPhones to enter SOS or SOS Only mode. While you should keep automatic network selection active, resetting it can fix connection issues if your phone has this feature:

1. Open Settings on your iPhone.

2. Select Cellular (or Mobile Service or Mobile Data, depending on your phone).

3. Select Network Selection (if available, not everyone will have this option).

4. Toggle Automatic off by tapping the slider so it turns gray.

5. Tap the slider again so it turns green.

Once you have completed these steps, your iPhone should be able to automatically select the best network without accidentally activating SOS or SOS Only mode. However, not all iPhones come with automatic network selection capabilities, so if you can't find a toggle under any tabs or windows, you will have to lift SOS or SOS Only mode using other methods.

Reset your iPhone's network settings

If nothing else works, you can reset your iPhone's network settings as a last resort. This process won't delete phone data such as photos and apps, but it will erase any connection data like Bluetooth devices saved for automatic connection, Wi-Fi passwords, and VPN data. So long as you have important info such as passwords backed up, you can begin resetting network settings:

1. Open Settings.

2. Select General.

3. Scroll all the way to the bottom of the page and select Transfer or Reset iPhone.

4. Select Reset.

5. Select Reset Network Settings, then confirm your choice.

Once the phone is done scrubbing its settings, it should be able to reconnect to networks without any SOS or SOS Only mode issues.

The above tutorials should resolve most SOS and SOS Only mode problems. But if you are still having trouble, you should contact official Apple product support.
